claims.claim: Pipecat input transport feeds an asyncio.Queue consumed by _audio_task_handler (base_input.py:226, 235)
LiveKit worker computes job_load = worker_load / len(active_jobs) (worker.py:778-780)
worker reports the computed job_load back to the LiveKit server
claims.found: job_load at line 780 is used locally to size idle processes (set_target_idle_processes), NOT sent to the server. What is reported to the server is self._worker_load: line 1438 'UpdateWorkerStatus(load=self._worker_load, status=status...)'
claims.correction: The value reported back to the server is worker_load (worker.py:1438), not job_load. job_load is an internal idle-process sizing input.

claims.claim: on drain the worker flips to WS_FULL so the server stops routing new jobs
LiveKit ConnectionPool bounds and reuses upstream provider sockets (connection_pool.py:24)
claims.found: 14: class ConnectionPool(Generic[T]): 'Helper class to manage persistent connections like websockets. Handles connection pooling and reconnection after max duration.' line 24 is max_session_duration param; reuse via _available set / async context manager returning conns to pool

claims.claim: Deepgram lists three chokepoints: WebSocket concurrency limits, Orchestrator throughput, Downstream LLM rate limits + prescribes async runtimes/load balancers w/ connection persistence, graceful draining and backpressure (ebook 759-770)
claims.found: 764-766: 'WebSocket concurrency limits / Orchestrator throughput / Downstream LLM rate limits' 767-768: 'Use async runtimes and load balancers that support connection persistence. Implement graceful connection draining and backpressure during traffic spikes.'

claims.claim: Model tiering: reserving larger models for complex turns and using lighter models for routine exchanges (ebook 914-916)
claims.found: 915-916: 'many deployments tier model usage, reserving larger models for complex turns and using lighter models for routine exchanges.' (LLM rate limit/cost context at 914)

claims.claim: Together AI autoscaler: scale up aggressively so requests never back up (together-talk 34)
claims.found: line 34: 'Auto-scaling: scale up aggressively (never let requests back up); scale down carefully ... must drain conversations to completion.'

summary: All source quotes (deepgram 764-768, 915-916; together-talk 34) and code_refs (pipecat base_input.py:226/235, livekit worker.py:778-780, connection_pool.py:24) verified accurate. One mechanism nuance is MISCITED: the note says the worker 'reports a computed job_load back to the LiveKit server'; job_load (worker.py:780) is used locally to size idle processes, while the value actually sent to the server is worker_load (worker.py:1438). No WRONG claims.
